meta.channel_note: English-language YouTube Shorts. Pixel-art history in 60 seconds narrated by a pixel raccoon mascot. Sister channel to RU original 'Киса Историк за Минуту'.
meta.usage: Every pipeline stage reads this file. Change values HERE, never inline in scripts.

character.name: History Raccoon
character.pinned_prompt: a cute chubby cartoon raccoon mascot named History Raccoon, gray fur with a black bandit mask around the eyes, bushy ringed tail, wearing a tiny brown leather explorer hat and a small red scarf, big friendly eyes, flat 2D 16-bit pixel art sprite, cel-shaded flat colors, clean dark outline, front-facing three-quarter view, retro SNES video game character
character.pinned_prompt_note: PINNED. NOTE: currently unused by the pipeline (mascot.enable=false; the raccoon is cast inside scenes via scene descriptions + visual_bible, and the thumbnail portrait references assets/mascot/raccoon-test.png directly). Kept as the canonical character string; style tail updated 2026-07-05 to match style.keywords.
character.negative: no photorealism, no 3d render, no motion blur, no jpeg artifacts, not modern flat vector, no realistic photo

style.keywords: flat 2D pixel art, 16-bit SNES JRPG cutscene style, cel-shaded flat color fills, clean dark outlines, fine pixel grid with subtle dithering, vibrant limited palette, hand-placed pixel detail
style.keywords_note: Reworked 2026-07-05 to match the reference Kisa channel: detailed painterly SNES-era pixel art (soft shading, glow, fine grid, rich color) NOT chunky NES pixels. Do NOT reintroduce 'NES palette', 'chunky pixels' or 'thick black outlines' -- the 32-colour posterized look was the rejected style; richness now comes from the 128-colour pixelate pass.
style.scene_prompt_template: {scene}. {style}. Strictly a FLAT 2D illustration like a retro video game cutscene: no 3D render, no realistic fur texture, no photorealism, no depth of field, no soft airbrush shading. no text, no words, no letters, no watermark, wide cinematic composition, vertical 9:16 framing
style.scene_prompt_template_note: {scene} = one scene description from episode.json. {style} = style.keywords. Character prompt is appended when the raccoon appears in the scene.

voice.engine_note: 'edge-tts' | 'elevenlabs' (default since 2026-07-04, key quota fixed). elevenlabs uses v3 emotion audio tags + returns character timestamps (tts.py builds words.json directly, align.py skips whisper). If elevenlabs fails at run time, run.py falls back to edge-tts with a loud warning so the pipeline never blocks.
voice.name: en-US-ChristopherNeural
voice.note: Free Microsoft neural voice. Christopher = warm documentary narrator. Rate +8% keeps ~155 words under 60s.

elevenlabs.model_note: Verified present + text-to-speech-capable on this key's tier via GET /v1/models (2026-07-04). tts.py resolves model_fallback against /v1/models at run time and uses the first available id; eleven_v3 was available so no fallback was needed.
elevenlabs.voice_note: Выбран Тимой 2026-07-06 из 7 сэмплов (см. assets/voice-samples/candidates-2026-07-06/): хриплый характерный голос-трикстер под персонажа енота-бандита — сильная отстройка от 'гладкого ИИ-диктора'. Запасной: Daniel onwK4e9ZLuTAKqWW03F9 (британский диктор). Прежний George JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb звучал 'как ИИ'.
elevenlabs.settings_note: 2026-07-06 humanize-ресерч + A/B Тимы: stability 0.4 (зона 0.35-0.45 — живая вариативность без галлюцинаций Creative-режима; Тима выбрал сэмпл A @0.3, 0.4 = безопасная версия того же) + style 0.2. similarity 0.75. Текст остаётся 'письменным' — устный рерайт не требуется.

postfx.note: Чистый voice polish (2026-07-12): highpass 70Hz + мягкий компрессор 2:1 + loudnorm I=-14/TP=-1.5, без искусственного room tone. Применяется к voice.mp3 ПОСЛЕ снятия таймстемпов (тайминг не сдвигает). Идемпотентно: оригинал сохраняется в voice.prefx.mp3, наличие prefx = уже обработано.

video.crf_note: Remotion x264 CRF. 23 = visually lossless-ish, keeps a 60s Short ~15-25MB instead of 100MB+. Lower=bigger/better.

captions.note: Kisa-style (2026-07-06, по покадровому разбору @kisahistory): ОДНО маленькое ЖЁЛТОЕ слово ВВЕРХУ кадра (top_offset_px от верха), активный акцент (цифры/emphasis) — белый. Раньше: гигантские белые 132px по центру-низу — закрывали кадр, главный визуальный шум.

script.note: Retuned 2026-07-11 from live channel analytics: target a 38-50 second final Short. Callum scripts should stay near 80 words and open with the physical outcome in the first sentence.

pixelate.note: Nearest-neighbor downscale to downscale_width then upscale back to video width, quantize to palette_colors. Forces a uniform pixel grid on ALL images (codex or placeholder). Retuned 2026-07-05 from 320/32 to 400/128 to match the Kisa reference: a 4-variant test on grok_anchor.png (320/32 vs 560/96 vs 720/160 vs raw) showed the crude look came from the 32-colour palette (heavy banding, mushed faces), NOT the grid coarseness -- Kisa's effective grid is ~300-360px. 400/128 keeps Kisa's visible-but-fine pixel grid while 128 colours restore painterly shading/glow and readable crowd detail. animate.py reuses these values for the shared-palette video pass (128-colour frame-0 palette still quantizes cleanly and stays temporally stable).

animate.enable_note: OFF 2026-07-06: hero clip is pre-generated via Vidu Q2 (fal-ai/vidu/q2/image-to-video/pro, movement small — validated in spec/video-generation-playbook.md) and placed as scenes/scene_4_video.mp4 manually; assemble stages any scene_N_video.mp4 it finds. Grok i2v is deprecated (morphing). Re-enable only after animate.py gets a vidu_q2 provider.
animate.mode_note: 'boomerang' (default ambient i2v loop) or 'flf' (first-last-frame ONE-WAY action clip). episode.json 'hero_mode' overrides per-episode. flf reads scene_(hero-1).png as the start frame and scene_hero.png as the end frame, plays once, then Remotion crossfades to the end still (no boomerang) so an action reads as a single forward motion.
animate.flf_providers_note: Tried in order for mode=flf. kling_o1 (Kling O1, $0.112/s, best action) first; wan_flf2v (Wan-2.1 FLF2V, ~$0.20-0.40) is the cheaper fallback if the Kling id 404s or errors.
animate.prompt_suffix: Subtle ambient motion ONLY: {motion}. NO new objects or events. Camera locked. PRESERVE the 16-bit pixel art style, hard pixel grid, no smoothing.
animate.flf_prompt: The white-kitted raccoon's diving header sends the leather ball flying past the yellow goalkeeper into the net; the net ripples; white-kitted raccoons begin celebrating. Flat 2D 16-bit pixel art cutscene style, clean outlines, no smoothing.
animate.default_motion_hints: gentle ambient sway, distant flags wave, soft light flicker
animate.note: animate.py: hero-scene image-to-video via fal.ai. boomerang mode uploads scenes/scene_N.png, runs i2v (grok=$0.25+$0.002/clip, wan turbo=$0.05 budget fallback, default provider grok), re-pixelates every frame against ONE SHARED palette (frame-0 palette -> no boiling), bakes a seamless forward+reverse boomerang. flf mode (mode='flf' or episode 'hero_mode'='flf') uploads scene_(hero-1).png + scene_hero.png as start+end keyframes to a first-last-frame model (kling_o1 -> wan_flf2v fallback), re-pixelates the same way, encodes FORWARD-ONLY (no boomerang; Remotion crossfades to the end still). Writes scenes/scene_N_video.mp4 (1080-wide, fps_out, no audio). HARD COST GUARD: never exceeds max_clips_per_episode NOR max_usd_per_episode (kling_o1 5s = $0.56). Resumable (skips existing mp4). NEVER blocks the pipeline: any API/timeout/download/ffmpeg failure warns loudly, removes partials, and the scene falls back to stills. Hero scene = episode.json 'hero_scene' (1-based) + 'hero_motion_hints', else a motion-keyword heuristic. Set enable=false to render stills only.

scenes_adapter.note: primary 'grok' | 'codex' | 'placeholder'. grok = fal.ai Grok Imagine with the CONTINUITY PROTOCOL: scene_1 via text-to-image (prompt + episode.json visual_bible + style keywords, raw kept as scenes/grok_anchor.png), scenes 2..N via the edit endpoint referencing the scene_1 anchor (plus the mascot sprite if a scene mentions the raccoon) so cast/uniforms/palette stay consistent across the whole episode. max_usd_per_episode caps BOTH the scene set and the mascot pose pack (each stage independently). Any grok failure falls back per-scene to codex (up to codex_scene_budget within codex_total_timeout_seconds) then the Pillow placeholder, so the pipeline NEVER blocks.

music.note: Background music volume tracks VO activity: duck_no_speech in the gaps, duck_under_speech under narration (word within +/-0.3s), duck_punchline for ~0.4s before the final sentence. If music.file is absent, pipeline renders WITHOUT music (must not fail).

sfx.note: 8-bit SFX pack synthesized by sfx.py (deterministic, idempotent) into assets/sfx/. Placed in Remotion at cold-open, scene transitions, keyword pops, snap punch and the punchline beat. Final gain = master_gain * per-type gain; WAVs are peak-normalized to 0.7, so the layer sits clearly under the untouched voice. Set enable=false to render without SFX.

mascot.enable_note: Corner narrator DISABLED since the kisahistory format rework: the raccoon acts INSIDE the scenes (cast via scene prompts + visual_bible). Set true to bring the corner sprite back.
mascot.note: Static-corner narrator, now animated. Prefers raccoon-test.png (codex) else raccoon.png. Optional talk-open/talk-half/blink frames drive the mouth flap; talk-half is auto-painted from test+open if absent.

motion.note: Motion pack. Every subsystem has an enable flag + amplitudes. Remotion reads these via props (run.py maps snake_case -> camelCase). The values below are the PUNCHY baseline; motion.style picks the profile Remotion resolves. Duration is NOT affected by any of this.
motion.style_note: 'smooth' (default) or 'punchy'. run.py passes this; Remotion (motionStyle.ts) folds the profile into one resolved config. smooth = same cut density, eased curves: shot-cut/sentence-start micro-shake off (scene-start 2px eased bump only), spring hook punch over ~7 frames, easeInOut camera moves, no-overshoot caption highlight, continuous mascot sway (no per-word squash), longer eased dissolve/wipe (~16f), softer scene-land impact. punchy = original hard-cut amplitudes below.

parallax.enable_note: ВЫКЛЮЧЕН НАВСЕГДА 2026-07-07 по вердикту Тимы: rembg-слои на пиксель-арте дают артефакты (призраки/пятна заплатки), «дешёвая анимация». Движение сцен = нейронный i2v (Vidu Q2) + Ken Burns. Не включать обратно.
parallax.fg_drift_multiplier_note: 2.7 -> 1.4 (2026-07-07): при 2.7 fg уезжал на ~43px и из-под него вылезал призрак из bg-заплатки (ep023 QA). Относительный сдвиг fg/bg теперь ~6px — под толщину пиксельной сетки.
parallax.note: layers.py cuts fg/bg per scene; bg scales bg_scale and drifts bg_drift_px, fg drifts fg_drift_multiplier x faster + a fg_zoom_pct slow zoom. Direction alternates per scene.

transitions.note: Rotated by scene index: pixel dissolve (0.4s), pixel wipe (0.35s), zoom-punch (0.3s). frames = max overlap; cell_px = dissolve/wipe grid. Overlaps neighbours, total duration unchanged.

effects.note: One effect per scene (particles / light rays / vignette-pulse), picked by episode.json 'motion' hint array if present else by scene index.

shake.note: 2-frame impact micro-shake at each scene start and on the first word of each sentence (word after a gap > sentence_gap_seconds).

captions.note: Active word springs 1.0 -> pop_scale -> settle_scale over ~4 frames. Each group slides up entrance_px + fades over entrance_frames.

mascot.note: Talking: closed->half->open->half flap at ~flap_hz (5 = deliberate, not chattering), +/-lean_deg lean sway, squash_pct squash-stretch per word. Idle: breath_pct breathing + blink. Slides up with one overshoot bounce over entrance_frames. Mouth flap only runs on the BASE narrator sprite; per-scene pose sprites (episode mascot/pose_scene_N.png from mascot_poses.py) replace it at scene boundaries with a small re-triggered entrance bounce and no mouth overlay.

shots.note: Multi-crop: each scene is hard-cut into 2-3 shots of the SAME image (wide -> detail punch-in -> alt crop) so the frame changes every 3-5s. Zoom never exceeds max_zoom (pixel grid stays chunky, never mushy). Each shot cut also fires a micro-shake + whoosh. Duration is NOT affected.
shots.note_disabled: OFF 2026-07-06: kisa-format эпизоды режутся на ~23 бит-сцены по 2-3с — мультикроп внутри такой сцены не нужен и дёргает. Для легаси-эпизодов с 5 сценами включить обратно.

hook.note: First-1.5s hook: cold-open title card slammed frames card_in..card_out (cleared before the snap punch so it never ghosts over it), first shot opens head_start_frac into its move (motion at t=0), snap punch-in (1.0->punch_scale) at punch_frame with micro-shake + whoosh + pop.

emphasis.note: Sparse keyword pops (max_pops per video): big accent word slam in the upper-middle zone (y_pct% from top, clear of captions/mascot). smooth style: soft scale-in over ~6f + hold + fade & drift-up over ~6f, faint 4% flash, synced blip SFX. Words: episode.json 'emphasis' array, else numbers/years then longest proper-nouns at sentence starts, min_spacing_seconds apart.

thumbnail.mode_note: 'portrait' (default, Kisa-style) | 'title'. portrait = ONE fal Grok-edit image of the mascot reacting (big emotive raccoon face + episode backdrop) with ONE huge burned word; title = the legacy composite (hero scene + wrapped title + corner mascot). episode.json 'thumbnail_mode' overrides. On any portrait-generation failure the stage falls back to 'title' so the pipeline never blocks.
thumbnail.hero_scene_note: Which scenes/scene_N.png to use as the thumbnail background (title mode). episode.json 'thumbnail_scene' overrides this per-episode.

portrait.edit_prompt_template: Dramatic close-up portrait of the EXACT same raccoon character as the reference image (same face markings, same brown flat cap, same fur), {emotion}, in front of {backdrop}, detailed 16-bit SNES-era pixel art, painterly pixel shading, soft glow, atmospheric cinematic lighting, rich color depth, fine pixel grid, no text, no words, no letters, no watermark, vertical 9:16 framing
portrait.default_backdrop: a dramatic atmospheric backdrop related to the episode, blurred, warm cinematic lighting
portrait.focus_y_wide_note: Wide (1280x720) crops a portrait source, so it zooms in hard. A higher focus_y keeps the emotive EYES + cap in the upper third (above the burned word) instead of framing only the muzzle.
portrait.note: One Grok edit (reference = assets/mascot/raccoon-test.png) -> out/thumb_portrait_base.png (cached/resumable), cover-cropped to both formats, re-pixelated, then ONE big word (episode.json 'thumbnail_word', else the punchiest short title word + '!') burned in huge pixel-style caps with a hard outline in the bottom third. Word is accent-colored if it contains a digit, else white. Cost-guarded against scenes_adapter.max_usd_per_episode (independent of the scene set).

thumbnail.wide_crop_focus_y_note: 0=top, 1=bottom, 0.5=center. Bias for the vertical->widescreen cover-crop since source scenes are portrait.

gradient.note: vertical: black ramps in from y = height*(1-vertical_bottom_start_frac) down to the bottom edge. wide: black ramps out from x=0 to x = width*wide_left_end_frac (roughly where the mascot sits, so it stays lit).

title.note: 3-5 punchiest words from episode.json title (text before first em-dash, minus stopwords). Accent color hits a number if one is present, else the last word.

mascot.note: Knocked-out raccoon composited at a corner with a subtle hard-edged pixel-style drop shadow (small blur, not a soft realistic shadow).

endcard.title: RACCOON REWIND
endcard.subtitle: NEW STORY EVERY DAY
endcard.note: Kisa-style closer после нарратива (2026-07-06): маскот + лого + SUBSCRIBE-пилюля + голосовая строка (assets/channel/endcard_voice.mp3, генерится один раз выбранным голосом; файла нет -> карта без голоса). Remotion: EndCard.tsx; длительность композиции растёт на seconds.
